findings.problem: The relapse purchase is staged in a neighborhood bodega selling half-pint vodka, which breaks contemporary NYC realism and conflicts with chapter-boundary handoff continuity that expects a liquor-store purchase context. This is a canonical world-logic contradiction, not a cosmetic detail.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the acquisition sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:209, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:211, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:217 so Marisol buys from a licensed liquor store (or change the substance to a legally sold bodega item). Keep downstream relapse beats intact but align location/object specifics with the chapter handoff.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised text in lines 207-233 clearly shows a legally plausible purchase path for the intoxicant and no bodega clerk is selling distilled liquor; FAIL if vodka/spirits are still purchased from a bodega or location details still contradict the handoff setup.

findings.problem: After the first-drink turning point, the chapter runs a long iterative loop of drafting/deleting messages and additional swallows without enough new external pressure, so escalation plateaus instead of compounding consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-sequence the post-relapse middle in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:273 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:315 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:393 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:416 by adding at least one concrete interruptive event (time-sensitive call consequence, missed-meeting threshold, or immediate practical fallout) that forces a fresh decision before the final unsent-thread beat.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after line 251, at least one new external event materially changes Marisol's available choices before the close at lines 449-453; FAIL if the section remains primarily iterative draft/delete self-looping with no new pressure variable.

findings.problem: Two incidental speakers (cashier and halal vendor) land similar cautionary mini-lectures in the same chapter movement, making side voices converge into one advisory authorial register rather than distinct neighborhood idiolects.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate exchange texture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:225 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:230 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:375 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:380: keep one interaction terse/procedural and let the other carry character-specific cadence or transactional motive, instead of two parallel cautionary lines.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each side character's speech is distinguishable by syntax/rhythm without dialogue tags and only one of the two exchanges delivers direct life-advice; FAIL if both still read as interchangeable warning one-liners to Marisol.

findings.problem: A recurring construction appears more than twice: the repeated "She typed:" plus "Backspace/Delete" loop (lines 277, 281, 285, 303, 329, 411 with delete markers at 279, 283, 301, 305). The repetition reads as mechanical patterning rather than escalating interior form.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:277 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:307 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:411 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:415 so only one or two explicit compose/delete beats remain; shift the remaining pressure into embodied action, sensory distortion, or decision cost.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage contains no more than two explicit "She typed:" lead-ins and no more than one standalone delete/backspace marker while preserving the unsent-confession conflict to both Luis and Ada; FAIL if three or more near-identical compose/delete beat pairs remain.

summary: Chapter 6 lands major causal beats (Gloria's reveal, on-page relapse, concealment from Luis/Ada) and generally holds close-third emotional pressure, but it fails review due to one HIGH continuity/world-logic contradiction plus unresolved structural/dialogue/prose flattening in the back half.
